tasks.subject: Task 1: LevelMeta.starting_abilities + maze declaration
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `LevelMeta` gains a defaulted `starting_abilities: frozenset[Ability]` parsed from an optional `"starting_abilities"` JSON array; `maze.json` declares `["double_jump"]`. Levels omitting the field default to an empty frozenset (back-compat).

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/blueball/levels/loader.py` (import `Ability`; add `LevelMeta` field; parse in `load_level`)
- Modify: `src/blueball/levels/maze.json` (add `"starting_abilities": ["double_jump"]` after `"spawn"`)
- Test: `tests/test_level_loader.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A level JSON without `starting_abilities` -> `meta.starting_abilities == frozenset()`.
- [ ] A level JSON with `["double_jump"]` -> `frozenset({Ability.DOUBLE_JUMP})`.
- [ ] `load_level("maze.json")` -> `Ability.DOUBLE_JUMP in meta.starting_abilities`.
- [ ] An unknown ability string raises `ValueError`.
- [ ] Pre-existing loader tests stay green.

**Verify:** `PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_level_loader.py -q` -> all pass; then full suite = 424.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/blueball/levels/loader.py", "src/blueball/levels/maze.json", "tests/test_level_loader.py"], "verifyCommand": "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_level_loader.py -q", "acceptanceCriteria": ["level without field -> starting_abilities == frozenset()", "level with [double_jump] -> frozenset({DOUBLE_JUMP})", "maze.json -> DOUBLE_JUMP in starting_abilities", "unknown ability string raises ValueError", "existing loader tests green"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Grant starting abilities in curriculum training
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `make_curriculum_player` gains a defaulted `abilities` param and grants it (as a mutable `set`) to the `Player`; `evaluate_curriculum` passes `meta.starting_abilities`. After this, a maze curriculum player (the box-lava specialist's path) has double jump.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/blueball/ai/curriculum.py` (`make_curriculum_player` signature + Player construction; the call in `evaluate_curriculum`)
- Test: `tests/test_ai_curriculum.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `make_curriculum_player(world, g, spawn, keys)` with no `abilities` -> `player.abilities == set()` (back-compat).
- [ ] `make_curriculum_player(..., frozenset({Ability.DOUBLE_JUMP}))` -> `DOUBLE_JUMP in player.abilities` and `in player.jump_ctrl.abilities`.
- [ ] `evaluate_curriculum` on maze passes `starting_abilities` (with DOUBLE_JUMP) into `make_curriculum_player`.
- [ ] `player.abilities` is a mutable `set` (so `unlock()` works), not a frozenset.

**Verify:** `PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_ai_curriculum.py -q` -> all pass; then full suite = 427.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/blueball/ai/curriculum.py", "tests/test_ai_curriculum.py"], "verifyCommand": "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_ai_curriculum.py -q", "acceptanceCriteria": ["default no-abilities player == set()", "frozenset({DOUBLE_JUMP}) -> player + jump_ctrl have it", "evaluate_curriculum on maze passes meta.starting_abilities", "player.abilities is a mutable set"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Grant starting abilities in static trainer.evaluate + TrainScene
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The authoritative static-level path (`trainer.evaluate`) and the cosmetic `TrainScene` display players both grant `set(meta.starting_abilities)`, so maze level-training trains (and shows) a double-jumping agent. `evaluate_infinite` is left unchanged (Infinite Run has no level meta).

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/blueball/ai/trainer.py` (static `evaluate` player spawn, ~line 93 — NOT `evaluate_infinite`)
- Modify: `src/blueball/scenes/train.py` (cosmetic players in `_start_generation`, ~line 133)
- Test: `tests/test_ai_smoke.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `trainer.evaluate` on maze constructs its `Player` with `abilities` containing `Ability.DOUBLE_JUMP`.
- [ ] `trainer.evaluate` on tutorial_hill (no starting abilities) -> `abilities == set()` (back-compat).
- [ ] A `TrainScene` built on maze has every `_players[i].abilities` containing `DOUBLE_JUMP`.
- [ ] `evaluate_infinite` unchanged (no abilities granted).

**Verify:** `PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_ai_smoke.py -q` -> all pass; then full suite = 430.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/blueball/ai/trainer.py", "src/blueball/scenes/train.py", "tests/test_ai_smoke.py"], "verifyCommand": "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_ai_smoke.py -q", "acceptanceCriteria": ["evaluate on maze -> Player abilities has DOUBLE_JUMP", "evaluate on tutorial_hill -> abilities == set()", "TrainScene on maze -> all _players have DOUBLE_JUMP", "evaluate_infinite unchanged"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: PlayScene unions level + save abilities (real-game parity)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `PlayScene` constructs the player with `save-unlocked ∪ level starting_abilities`, so loading a level directly (even with an empty save) honors the level's declared abilities. Maze player has double jump regardless of save state.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/blueball/scenes/play.py` (`_reset`, the `Player(...)` construction)
- Test: `tests/test_play_scene.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Loading maze with an empty save -> `scene.player.abilities` contains `Ability.DOUBLE_JUMP`.
- [ ] Loading maze with a save unlocking double jump -> still contains `DOUBLE_JUMP` (union, no error).
- [ ] Loading tutorial_hill with an empty save -> `scene.player.abilities == set()` (existing test stays green).

**Verify:** `PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_play_scene.py -q` -> all pass; then full suite = 432.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/blueball/scenes/play.py", "tests/test_play_scene.py"], "verifyCommand": "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_play_scene.py -q", "acceptanceCriteria": ["maze + empty save -> player has DOUBLE_JUMP", "maze + save double_jump -> union has DOUBLE_JUMP", "tutorial_hill + empty save -> abilities == set()"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Retrain box-lava specialist & observe verdict (USER-ORDERED GATE)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** With the double-jump fix in place, retrain the box-lava specialist and observe whether it now reaches the goal — the user-ordered "retrain first" check that decides whether the reserved cradle + on-box-jump reward are still needed.

**USER-ORDERED GATE — NON-SKIPPABLE.** This task was requested by the user in the current conversation ("grant double jump, retrain first… add cradle only if it still struggles"). It MUST NOT be closed by walking around it, by declaring it "verified inline", or by substituting a cheaper check. Close only after the retrain has actually run end-to-end and the verdict line + `run.json` `cracked` have been captured.

**Files:** none (empirical run; no code/tests).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Retrain runs to completion (exit 0) at spec defaults (pop 80 x gens 200, world=1, ga=0).
- [ ] The stdout `Verdict @ 'box_lava': reached_goal=<bool> fitness=<float>` line is captured.
- [ ] The new run's `run.json` `curriculum.cracked` is recorded.
- [ ] Compared vs single-jump baseline (reached_goal=False, fitness 1020.5, cracked=false). DECISION: reached_goal=True -> feature done; still False -> escalate to reserved cradle + on-box-jump reward (separate spec/plan).

**Verify:** `PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python train_maze_curriculum.py --box-lava` -> exits 0; capture `Verdict @ 'box_lava': reached_goal=…` and newest `genomes/mazeboxlavacurr_w1_*/run.json` `cracked`.

```json:metadata
{"userGate": true, "tags": ["user-gate"], "requiresUserSpecification": false, "files": [], "verifyCommand": "PYTHONPATH=$PWD/src /home/ddgg0/projects/BlueBall/.venv/bin/python train_maze_curriculum.py --box-lava", "acceptanceCriteria": ["retrain exits 0 at pop80xgens200 world=1", "stdout Verdict reached_goal=<bool> captured", "run.json curriculum.cracked recorded", "compared vs baseline (False/1020.5/cracked=false); decision recorded"]}
```
